title: Inflammatory signals from fatty bone marrow support DNMT3A driven clonal hematopoiesis
research_question: Does fatty bone marrow (FBM) modify the evolution of clonal hematopoiesis (CH) driven by DNMT3A mutations?
methods_summary: Establishment of FBM models in NSG mice via sub-lethal irradiation or castration, with PPARγ inhibitor as control
Transplantation of human DNMT3A R882H-mutated preleukemic HSPCs and normal CD34+ cells into FBM mice
Transplantation of DNMT3A-mutant (R882H knock-in) and wild-type murine BM cells into FBM mice, including secondary transplants to assess self-renewal
Single-cell RNA-sequencing (MARS-Seq and 10X Genomics) of LK cells from DNMT3A-mutant and wild-type mice exposed to FBM or normal BM
Cytokine multiplex assay (FirePlex) of BM fluid and serum from FBM and control mice
In vitro co-culture of DNMT3A-mutant HSPCs with adipocytes or MSCs, and colony-forming cell (CFC) assays with IL-6 supplementation
In vivo treatment with neutralizing IL-6 antibodies in FBM mice transplanted with DNMT3A-mutant cells
dataset: Human AML samples (e.g., #160005, #141464) and cord blood/mobilized peripheral blood; murine DNMT3A R882H knock-in, DNMT3A haploinsufficient, and SRSF2 P95H knock-in models; NSG and NSG-SGM3 mice
key_findings: FBM provides a selective advantage to human and murine DNMT3A-mutant HSPCs, increasing engraftment and self-renewal, but not to wild-type HSPCs or SRSF2-mutant cells
DNMT3A-mutant cells exposed to FBM maintain their HSC pool and show 6-10 fold increase in HSCs compared to controls, with activation of inflammatory signaling (IFN-γ, IL-6)
FBM secretes IL-6 (and other cytokines) in BM fluid; IL-6 levels are elevated in FBM and reduced by PPARγ inhibitor
In vitro, IL-6 enhances self-renewal of DNMT3A-mutant HSPCs in serial replating assays; co-culture with adipocytes increases colony formation
In vivo, neutralizing IL-6 antibodies significantly reduce the selective advantage of DNMT3A-mutant cells under FBM conditions
extracted_insights: Paracrine inflammatory signals from fatty bone marrow, particularly IL-6, promote the self-renewal and selective advantage of DNMT3A-mutant hematopoietic stem cells, driving clonal hematopoiesis. This effect can be inhibited by blocking the IL-6 pathway, suggesting a potential therapeutic target to mitigate age-related clonal hematopoiesis and leukemia risk.
